LLMs, Go Code! — a BookBank field guide Contents Cheatsheet

A BookBank Field Guide · Go & Agentic Coding

LLMs, Go Code!

Designing, prompting, and auditing Go systems
in the age of coding agents.

Told in the voice of The Crafter — calm, incremental, Crafting-Interpreters style.
“If your agent tries to write something clever, it is fighting the language.”

The Path

What we'll cover

Go's whole design — small vocabulary, explicit errors, structural typing, a strict compiler — happens to make it one of the easiest languages for an LLM to write correctly, and one of the most important for you to be able to read, direct, and audit. Ten chapters, each teaching the real Go concept first, then “the agent angle.”

Part I · The Go Blueprint

  1. The Zen of GoWhy boring is a superpower
  2. Structs & CompositionModel data before the agent codes
  3. Implicit InterfacesSandboxes for agent-generated code
  4. Errors as ValuesCatching a corner-cutting agent
  5. Goroutines, Channels & ContextWhere agents fail most
  6. The Go ToolchainAn automated feedback loop

Part II · Directing the Agent

  1. Executable SpecificationsPrompts, interfaces & schemas
  2. Test-Driven Agent DevelopmentRed, green, refactor — with an LLM

Part III · Built With an Agent

  1. Case Study: an API ServiceHuman architect, agent implementer
  2. Case Study: a CLI ToolPrompting flags & OS interaction
  3. CheatsheetEvery audit checklist, one page
How to use this book Every chapter ends with an audit checklist — the concrete things to check in code an agent handed you. Turn pages with the ← → arrows (or the buttons below). Tap ↑ to jump back here.
